behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a module-handoff risk: history management + prompt-builder + LLM client payload wiring. Unit tests on trimming logic alone can miss wiring bugs (wrong field, wrong ordering, off-by-one at boundary). Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess, no requirement for real model judgment quality, and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real request assembly path with 11+ prior messages using a stub LLM transport that captures the outbound payload; assert exactly the latest 10 messages (correct order/roles) are sent.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is necessary because the assertion is about cross-module payload handoff, not a single pure function.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality/relevance; stubs can only return canned on-topic answers and hide failure. Workflow is disqualified by behavior text because it asks about one generation outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real model calls on a representative prompt set and score topical relevance/answerability against acceptance criteria (human rubric or validated LLM-judge), with repeat runs to account for nondeterminism.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly evaluates model output quality, which requires Agentic.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is model-dependent tone judgment (too formal vs acceptable). Mocking the tone checker hides exactly this failure. Workflow was considered, but disqualified by behavior text: it requires one model decision (tone flag) and one orchestration consequence (trigger regen), not validation of multiple agentic decisions’ combined quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic casual-tier cases through the real tone-check path; verify the checker flags over-formal drafts and that regeneration is invoked when flagged.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions rely on real model judgment for tone, so Agentic is the lowest defensible level.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module persistence behavior (chat flow + persistence layer + timestamp handling) and is well-covered by integration with a real/local DB. System is disqualified by behavior text: no explicit requirement for real HTTP/external API/subprocess contract, only DB round-trip within app boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a conversation that includes regeneration against a test database; read back persisted rows and assert all turns are present, ordered, linked, and timestamped correctly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires module wiring and DB round-trip beyond unit, but not real external infrastructure.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: This is an externally visible side-effect guarantee ('customer never sees'). To avoid false confidence, you must verify the actual customer-facing boundary (response/stream/UI/API) via round-trip observation. Integration-only internal assertions can miss leakage at transport/presentation layers. Agentic/Workflow are not strictly required by the text if using deterministic forcing of the regeneration path; the essential requirement is external visibility proof.
behaviors.test_description: Drive the app through its real serving interface (e.g., HTTP or streaming endpoint) in a case where regeneration occurs; assert the client-observed output contains only the regenerated response and never exposes the rejected draft.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System is required because the pass condition is externally visible behavior at the customer boundary.
